model_narrative: We plan shipments of a product from a set of canning plants to a set of markets. For each plant-to-market arc we decide how many cases to ship and whether to use that arc at all. Shipping incurs a per-case transport cost that varies by arc, and using an arc at all incurs a one-time fixed charge specific to that arc. The objective is to minimize the total transportation cost, combining the per-case shipping costs and the fixed charges of the arcs that are used.

sets.name: i
sets.doc: the set of canning plants that can ship product, each a supply origin

sets.name: j
sets.doc: the set of markets that receive product, each a demand destination

params.name: a
params.doc: the production capacity of each plant, in cases; the most that plant can ship out across all markets

params.name: b
params.doc: the demand at each market, in cases; the amount that market must receive

params.name: c
params.doc: the per-case transport cost on the arc from a plant to a market, in thousands of dollars per case

params.name: fixcost
params.doc: the fixed charge incurred on a plant-to-market arc whenever that arc is used at all, in thousands of dollars; charged once regardless of volume

params.name: minshipping
params.doc: the minimum number of cases that must flow on any arc that is used, in cases; a single scalar applying to every arc

params.name: bigM
params.doc: a single large constant, set to the largest plant capacity, used to cap flow on an arc to zero when that arc is not used; a scalar

vars.name: x
vars.doc: the shipment quantity sent on each plant-to-market arc, in cases

vars.name: use
vars.doc: arc-use indicator; equals 1 if the plant-to-market arc carries any shipment and 0 otherwise

vars.name: z
vars.doc: the total transportation cost over all arcs, in thousands of dollars

constraints.name: supply
constraints.intent: Each plant can ship out no more than its production capacity. For every plant, the total amount shipped from it across all markets must not exceed that plant's capacity.

constraints.name: demand
constraints.intent: Every market must receive at least the amount it demands. For each market, the total amount shipped into it from all plants must be at least its demand.

constraints.name: minship
constraints.intent: If an arc is used to ship product, the amount sent on it must meet a minimum shipping level. For each plant-to-market arc, the amount shipped must be at least the minimum shipping quantity whenever that arc is marked as used, and may be nothing when the arc is not used.

constraints.name: maxship
constraints.intent: No product may flow on an arc unless that arc is marked as used. For each plant-to-market arc, the amount shipped must be zero when the arc is not used, and otherwise may go up to a large cap that does not otherwise restrict the flow.

constraints.name: cost
constraints.intent: The total transportation cost gathers both the per-case shipping costs and the one-time fixed charges. Across every plant-to-market arc, value the cases shipped at that arc's per-case transport cost and add the arc's fixed charge whenever the arc is used. Set the total cost equal to the sum of these amounts over all arcs.

query: There's a proposal on the table to run the Seattle plant at its full nameplate so it isn't sitting partly idle. If we require Seattle's total outbound shipments to hit at least 350 cases, what's the resulting freight bill?
expected_pyomo: model.sea_full = Constraint(expr=sum(model.x['seattle', j] for j in model.j) >= 350)
